text: fat is 6.1g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
carbs is 64.2g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
protein is 14.1g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
vitamin e at 2% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 49% DV, iron at 25% DV, zinc at 18% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first. unusually strong protein case for a grain because designed to expose whether grain proteins rescue too hard. good mineral support because better than many grains. strong healthy-grain reputation because very likely audience debate food.
the drawbacks next. GI is only decent because not elite on carb behaviour. prep quality matters because can be unpleasant if badly prepared. can be expensive vs staples because practical downside.
B tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
